facts.text: Olean earned the nickname 'Little Chicago' in the press because mobsters and bootleggers frequented the town, including a regular visit from Capone.
The Penn Central railroad ran a passenger train through Olean until May 1, 1971, when Amtrak’s arrival ended regional service.
The Olean Historical & Preservation Society offers three free locations filled with local history and memorabilia for visitors to explore.
In 1804 Adam Hoops, an agent of the Holland Land Company, purchased the land that would become the Town and City of Olean.
Bonaventure University, founded just outside Olean in 1858, remains a notable higher‑education institution in the region.
